Guides

Paddles & Gear

Choosing pickleball gear is mostly about matching equipment to how you actually play, not chasing the most expensive option. These guides compare paddles, shoes, and balls on the attributes that change how the game feels — weight, grip size, core, and face material — and explain the trade-offs in plain terms so you can decide for yourself.

Paddles & Gear

What to know before buying an expensive pickleball paddle

What a premium paddle adds, where returns diminish, and when an upgrade is genuinely worth it versus when a mid-range paddle is plenty.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Paddles & Gear

Pickleball paddles for tennis players switching over

How a tennis background shapes paddle choice, the habits to adjust, and why the soft game matters more than the power you bring.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Paddles & Gear

Choosing a pickleball paddle for 3.5 players

What a 3.5 player should look for in a paddle — control, spin, and a comfortable swing weight — and why chasing pure power usually backfires.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Paddles & Gear

Control vs power paddles: which one suits you

What makes a paddle lean toward control or power, the trade-offs of each, and how to choose the side of the spectrum that fits your game.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Paddles & Gear

Paddle face materials, compared: carbon fiber, fiberglass, hybrid

A like-for-like comparison of the common paddle face materials and who each one suits.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Paddles & Gear

How to choose your first pickleball paddle

A buying-decision guide that explains the four attributes that actually change how a paddle plays, so you can match one to your game.

Updated Jun 21, 2026
Paddles & Gear

Why court shoes matter — what to look for (and avoid)

What separates court shoes from running shoes and how to choose for hard or indoor courts.

How to assemble a complete beginner kit and where it is worth spending a little more.
